resolutions.action: Restated the George & Williams (2003) finding to accurately reflect that the 18% figure refers to externally assessed functional acceptance test pass rates, not an internal count of tests written, and added clarifying context.

Replaced the informal inline source list with a full References section using consistent APA-style formatting including journal, volume, issue, page range, and DOI for each source. Added entries for the two new post-2010 studies.
Named the red-green-refactor cycle explicitly in the Introduction immediately after describing the three steps, aligning with standard TDD literature terminology.
Expanded the CI/CD recommendation in Section 5 with one actionable sentence on test pyramid composition to guide teams on granularity within a pipeline.

Added a qualifier to the 'living documentation' bullet in Section 3 to note that this benefit depends on tests being written at the behavioral level, connecting it to the caveat already present in Section 5.
Updated Executive Summary defect reduction figure from '40–80%' to '40–90%' to match Nagappan et al. (2008) as cited in Section 3 and the evidence table.

Expanded the evidence table with two post-2010 sources: Rafique & Mišić (2013) meta-analysis and Causevic et al. (2012) systematic review, to broaden the evidence base beyond the original three dated studies.
